It seems like clicking on the left panel moved the T-shape and changed the selected box. This is a significant finding!

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the current state after game over
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
print(f"Score: {last_action_result.get('score')}")

# Check what changed
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nBox selection (row 55): '{lines[55][6:61]}'")
print(f"Right H (row 33): '{lines[33][33:61]}'")
print(f"Right V top (row 35): '{lines[35][33:61]}'")

# Check T-shape position
t_shape_rows = []
for r in range(7, 31):
    row_str = lines[r][6:31]
    if 'c' in row_str:
        c_positions = [i+6 for i, c in enumerate(row_str) if c == 'c']
        t_shape_rows.append((r, c_positions))
print(f"\nT-shape positions: {t_shape_rows}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
